title: Fewer than 3 in 10 register for HMRC's Making Tax Digital shake-up

contentText: Most sole traders and landlords ignore marketing campaigns, though fines are coming Fewer than three-tenths of those required to sign up for quarterly software-based Making Tax Digital (MTD) reporting for the latest tax year that started this month have done so, according to HM Revenue & Customs.…

title: Arm CEO Haas on Shifting From Smartphones to AI
snippet: Arm CEO Rene Haas speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie as the chip company positions itself for a major shift beyond smartphones, betting big on the rapid growth of cloud computing and data centers. This interview was re…
contentText: Arm CEO Rene Haas speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie as the chip company positions itself for a major shift beyond smartphones, betting big on the rapid growth of cloud computing and data centers. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Boom Driving $100 Bln Chip Opportunity, Arm CEO Says
snippet: Chip company Arm is positioning itself for a major shift beyond smartphones, betting big on the rapid growth of cloud computing and data centers. CEO Rene Haas says the company expects this segment to dominate its busin…
contentText: Chip company Arm is positioning itself for a major shift beyond smartphones, betting big on the rapid growth of cloud computing and data centers. CEO Rene Haas says the company expects this segment to dominate its business in the years ahead. Haas speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie about the lucrative opportunity. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Arm CEO on Proposed California Wealth Tax: Not a Good Thing
snippet: Arm CEO Rene Haas warns a proposed wealth tax in California could drive top entrepreneurs out of Silicon Valley. "I don't think it's a good thing if it drives really smart people outside the valley," Haas tells Bloomber…
contentText: Arm CEO Rene Haas warns a proposed wealth tax in California could drive top entrepreneurs out of Silicon Valley. "I don't think it's a good thing if it drives really smart people outside the valley," Haas tells Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Arm CEO Says AI Is 'Much Bigger' Than the Internet Shift
snippet: Arm CEO Rene Haas says the artificial intelligence boom is not just another tech cycle, but rather something "much bigger" and more far-reaching than anything that came before. In an interview with Bloomberg's Tom Macke…
contentText: Arm CEO Rene Haas says the artificial intelligence boom is not just another tech cycle, but rather something "much bigger" and more far-reaching than anything that came before. In an interview with Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie, Haas explains why AI is scaling beyond the PC and mobile eras, driven by surging demand for compute, memory and infrastructure. This interview was recorded on March 23 for "Bloomberg Tech: Europe." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Stocks in Asia Lure Back Global Funds as War Tensions Ease
snippet: As tensions in the Middle East begin to ease, global investors are turning their attention back to the artificial intelligence trade, funneling fresh funds into Asian tech stocks after weeks of selling.
contentText: As tensions in the Middle East begin to ease, global investors are turning their attention back to the artificial intelligence trade, funneling fresh funds into Asian tech stocks after weeks of selling.

title: Chip Industry Week In Review
snippet: Intel teams up everywhere; GPU rowhammer attack; fast verification; Taiwan IC industry wants stockpiles; China poaches Taiwan talent; thinnest GaN chiplet; AFM-IR; Europe revenue drop; new edge design; BMW hydrogen powe…
contentText: Deals, Funding Intel will join Elon Musk’s Terafab chip manufacturing project alongside Tesla, SpaceX , and xAI . Intel described its role as helping refactor silicon fab technology for a project targeting production of 1 TW/year of compute for AI and robotics applications. Intel and Google are expanding a multi-year collaboration on AI and cloud infrastructure, with Intel Xeon processors continuing to power Google Cloud systems. Also, the two companies are expanding the co-development of custom ASIC-based IPUs. Broadcom will develop and supply Google with future TPUs , networking, and other components. Anthropic , Google , and Broadcom also inked a deal for multiple gigawatts of next-gen TPU capacity scheduled to come online in 2027, mostly located in the U.S. SiFive raised $400B toward…

contentText: Hours after the United States charged a Super Micro Computer Inc. co-founder with illegally smuggling billions of dollars’ worth of Nvidia Corp. AI chips to China, shares of a little-known Shenzhen-based computing company plummeted by the daily limit of 20%.

title: Adam Back Denies He Is Anonymous Bitcoin Founder Satoshi Nakamoto
snippet: Adam Back, Co-Founder and CEO of Blockstream, denied claims that he is the mysterious and anonymous founder of bitcoin after a New York Times investigation tied him to the creation of the currency. Back said that it's '…
contentText: Adam Back, Co-Founder and CEO of Blockstream, denied claims that he is the mysterious and anonymous founder of bitcoin after a New York Times investigation tied him to the creation of the currency. Back said that it's 'natural' that people are curious but that since Nakamoto stopped posting on online forums there have been few 'digital breadcrumbs' to point people towards an answer. (Source: Bloomberg)
